name: Chocolate Mint Swirl Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Generously grease and flour a 12-cup bundt cake pan.
2. In a small bowl, beat cream cheese on high speed until smooth and fluffy. Add granulated sugar, 2 egg yolks, 1/8 teaspoon peppermint extract, and 3 drops of green food coloring until smooth. Set aside.
3. In a large bowl, combine the cake mix, 1-1/3 cups water, oil, 2 whole eggs, and 2 egg whites. Mix on low speed for 30 seconds, then on medium speed for 2 minutes.
4. Pour half of the batter (about 2-1/2 cups) into the prepared pan. Spread the reserved cream cheese mixture in a ring about 1 inch wide at the center of the batter.
5. Pour the remaining batter over the cream cheese layer. Bake for 45 to 50 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the cake comes out clean. Allow cooling in the pan for 5 minutes.
6. Invert the cake onto a wire rack and remove the pan. Cool the cake completely.
7. Heat chocolate chips and shortening until melted. Mix powdered sugar, 1/4 teaspoon peppermint extract, 3 drops of green food coloring, corn syrup, and enough water to make a thick glaze that can be easily drizzled. Drizzle the glaze over the cooled cake.
8. Using a toothpick, create swirls in the glaze. Spoon the melted chocolate over the glaze in a half-inch wide ring. Refrigerate until serving time. Store any remaining cake in the refrigerator.
